metadata.name: SOX IT General Controls
metadata.description: The Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) IT General Controls (ITGC) framework addresses the IT control requirements under Sections 302 and 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002. ITGCs provide the foundation for reliable automated controls and data integrity in financial reporting systems, covering access management, change management, computer operations, and program development.
metadata.source_organization: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) / PCAOB
metadata.classification_reasoning: SOX is a U.S. federal law requiring public companies to establish internal controls over financial reporting. ITGC compliance is assessed annually by external auditors under PCAOB standards as part of the integrated audit of financial statements and internal controls.
metadata.framework_purpose: To ensure the reliability and integrity of IT systems that support financial reporting, providing assurance that automated controls, system-generated reports, and electronically stored financial data are accurate, complete, and properly authorized.
metadata.framework_scope: All IT systems, applications, databases, and infrastructure that support financial reporting processes for publicly traded companies subject to SEC reporting requirements.
framework_objectives: Ensure appropriate access controls over financially significant applications and data
Maintain controlled and authorized changes to IT systems supporting financial reporting
Ensure reliable and continuous computer operations including job scheduling and data backup
Enforce proper software development lifecycle controls for financial applications
Maintain segregation of duties across IT and business functions
Support entity-level controls and IT governance over financial reporting
Ensure integrity and accuracy of system-generated reports and information produced by the entity (IPE)
Maintain reliable financial close and reporting processes
metadata.target_audience: Public company IT management, Internal Audit, External Auditors, CFOs, CIOs, SOX Compliance Teams, IT Security Teams, and Application Owners responsible for financial reporting systems
metadata.compliance_deadline: Annual, aligned with fiscal year-end financial reporting
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Material weakness disclosures, adverse audit opinions, SEC enforcement actions, stock price decline, personal liability for CEO/CFO under Section 302 certifications, potential criminal penalties for willful non-compliance.
adoption_approach: Identify financially significant applications and IT systems in scope
Develop Risk and Control Matrices (RCMs) for each ITGC domain
Design and implement controls with proper documentation
Perform walkthroughs and test design effectiveness
Test operating effectiveness throughout the audit period
Evaluate and remediate deficiencies before year-end
Support external auditor testing and issue management report

controls.title: Logical Access Policy and Standards
controls.description: Formal policies and standards governing logical access to IT systems supporting financial reporting are documented, approved by management, and communicated to relevant personnel.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains documented logical access policies and standards that define access management requirements for all financially significant IT systems. Policies cover account provisioning, authentication requirements, access reviews, and privileged access management. Policies are reviewed and approved annually by appropriate management and communicated to all IT personnel and system owners.

controls.title: User Access Provisioning
controls.description: Access to financially significant applications, databases, and operating systems is provisioned through a formal request and approval process with documented business justification.
controls.full_text: New user access to in-scope applications, databases, and operating systems is granted only through a formal access request process. Requests require documented business justification and approval by the appropriate data owner or management authority before access is provisioned. Access rights are assigned based on job role and the principle of least privilege.
evidence_requirements: Sample of provisioned accounts with matching approvals

controls.title: User Access Modification
controls.description: Changes to user access rights due to role changes, transfers, or additional responsibilities are formally requested, approved, and processed in a timely manner.
controls.full_text: When a user's role or responsibilities change (e.g., transfer, promotion, change in job function), their access rights are formally reviewed and modified to reflect the new role. Modifications require documented approval from the new manager or data owner. Previous access rights that are no longer required are promptly removed.

controls.title: User Access Termination / De-provisioning
controls.description: Access for terminated employees and contractors is revoked in a timely manner upon separation from the organization.
controls.full_text: Upon notification of an employee or contractor termination, access to all in-scope applications, databases, operating systems, and network resources is revoked within a defined timeframe (typically within 24 hours for involuntary terminations and the last day of employment for voluntary separations). The termination process includes disabling or removing accounts, revoking VPN/remote access, and collecting physical access credentials.
evidence_requirements: Timeliness comparison of termination date vs. account disable date
Sample of terminated users verified as disabled

controls.title: Periodic User Access Reviews
controls.description: Management performs periodic reviews of user access to financially significant applications to validate that access rights remain appropriate and aligned with current job responsibilities.
controls.full_text: At least quarterly (or as defined by policy), application owners and management review user access listings for all in-scope applications, databases, and operating systems. Reviews validate that each user's access level is appropriate for their current role, that terminated users have been removed, and that privileged access is justified. Reviewers sign off on the access listing, and any inappropriate access identified is remediated promptly.
evidence_requirements: Completed access review listings with sign-off

controls.title: Privileged Access Management
controls.description: Privileged (administrative) access to IT systems is restricted, formally approved, and subject to enhanced monitoring and periodic review.
controls.full_text: Administrative and privileged access to operating systems, databases, network devices, and applications supporting financial reporting is restricted to authorized personnel with a documented business need. Privileged account assignments require approval from IT management and the information security function. Privileged access is reviewed at least quarterly, and activities performed under privileged accounts are logged and monitored.
evidence_requirements: Quarterly privileged access review with sign-off

controls.title: Password and Authentication Controls
controls.description: Password policies and authentication mechanisms enforce minimum complexity, length, expiration, and lockout requirements for all in-scope systems.
controls.full_text: Password and authentication configuration settings for in-scope applications, databases, and operating systems enforce minimum requirements including: minimum password length (8+ characters), complexity requirements (uppercase, lowercase, numeric, special characters), password expiration (90 days or less), password history (prevent reuse of last 12 passwords), and account lockout after defined failed attempts. Multi-factor authentication is required for remote access and privileged accounts.

controls.title: Physical Access Controls to Data Centers
controls.description: Physical access to data centers, server rooms, and facilities housing financially significant IT infrastructure is restricted to authorized personnel.
controls.full_text: Physical access to data centers, server rooms, network closets, and other facilities housing IT infrastructure supporting financial reporting is restricted through physical access control mechanisms (badge readers, biometrics, key locks). Access lists are maintained and reviewed periodically. Visitor access requires escort and logging. Physical access is revoked upon termination or role change that no longer requires facility access.

controls.title: Service Account and Generic Account Management
controls.description: Service accounts, generic accounts, and shared accounts used by applications and batch processes are inventoried, secured, and subject to enhanced controls.
controls.full_text: All service accounts, generic accounts, and shared accounts in in-scope systems are inventoried with documented owners and business purposes. Passwords for service accounts are complex, changed periodically (or managed via vault), and not shared or stored in plain text. Interactive login for service accounts is restricted where possible. Usage of shared and generic accounts is minimized and requires compensating controls such as additional logging.
evidence_requirements: Password management procedures for service accounts
Compensating controls documentation for shared accounts

controls.title: Database and Direct Data Access Restrictions
controls.description: Direct access to production databases and data is restricted and monitored to prevent unauthorized changes to financial data.
controls.full_text: Direct access to production databases containing financial data (via SQL query tools, database management utilities, or command-line interfaces) is restricted to authorized database administrators. Direct data modifications (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on financial tables) require formal approval and are logged. Application users access data only through the application interface with appropriate application-level controls.
evidence_requirements: Direct data change approval and logging evidence
Application vs. direct access segregation evidence

controls.title: Change Management Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Formal change management policies and procedures govern all changes to in-scope applications, databases, operating systems, and infrastructure components.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains documented change management policies and procedures that define the process for requesting, evaluating, approving, testing, implementing, and reviewing changes to IT systems supporting financial reporting. The policy covers all change types including application code changes, configuration changes, infrastructure changes, emergency changes, and patch deployments. Policies are reviewed and approved annually.

controls.title: Change Request and Documentation
controls.description: All changes to in-scope systems are formally requested, documented with business justification, and tracked through a change management system.
controls.full_text: Changes to in-scope applications, databases, and infrastructure are initiated through formal change requests (tickets) that include: description of the change, business justification, risk assessment, affected systems, rollback plan, and testing requirements. All changes are logged and tracked in a change management system from initiation through closure.
evidence_requirements: Change request tickets with required fields

controls.title: Change Authorization and Approval
controls.description: Changes are reviewed and approved by appropriate management and technical stakeholders before implementation in the production environment.
controls.full_text: All changes to in-scope systems require documented approval from appropriate authorities before implementation. Approvals include: business/functional owner approval for business logic changes, technical review and approval for code and configuration changes, and Change Advisory Board (CAB) approval for significant or high-risk changes. Approvals are documented in the change management system prior to production deployment.
evidence_requirements: Change approval records in ticketing system
CAB meeting minutes for significant changes
Sample of changes with approval timestamps before deployment

controls.title: Change Testing and Quality Assurance
controls.description: Changes are tested in a non-production environment and validated for functional correctness, data integrity, and security before deployment to production.
controls.full_text: All changes to in-scope systems are tested in a segregated development or QA environment before production deployment. Testing includes functional testing, regression testing, user acceptance testing (UAT), and security testing as appropriate. Test results are documented, and sign-off from the business owner or designated tester is obtained before production migration. Testing environments are configured to reasonably replicate production settings.

controls.title: Segregation of Duties in Change Management
controls.description: Individuals who develop or modify code do not have the ability to migrate those changes to the production environment, ensuring segregation of duties.
controls.full_text: The organization enforces segregation of duties in the change management process such that the individual who develops, codes, or configures a change is not the same individual who migrates or implements that change in the production environment. This segregation is enforced through access controls that restrict developer access to production environments and migration tools. Where system limitations prevent technical enforcement, compensating detective controls (such as independent review of migration logs) are implemented.
evidence_requirements: Developer vs. migrator access privilege reports
Migration log review for SoD compliance

controls.title: Emergency Change Procedures
controls.description: Emergency changes follow an expedited but controlled process with post-implementation documentation, approval, and review.
controls.full_text: Emergency changes required to resolve critical production issues follow a defined expedited process that allows for rapid implementation while maintaining appropriate controls. Emergency changes require verbal or email approval from an authorized approver before implementation, with formal documentation completed within a defined timeframe (typically 24-48 hours) after implementation. Post-implementation review validates that the emergency change was appropriate, properly tested, and documented.
evidence_requirements: Emergency change tickets with retroactive approvals

controls.title: Production Migration Controls
controls.description: Migration of changes to production environments follows a controlled process with restricted access and audit trail.
controls.full_text: The migration of approved and tested changes to the production environment is performed by authorized personnel (separate from developers per SoD requirements) following a defined deployment procedure. Migration tools and production environment access are restricted to authorized migration personnel. All production deployments are logged with timestamps, migrator identity, and change reference. Automated deployment pipelines enforce approval gates before production release.
evidence_requirements: Deployment logs with change ticket references
Automated pipeline configuration with approval gates

controls.title: Configuration and Version Control
controls.description: Source code and configurations are maintained in version control systems with complete change history and baseline management.
controls.full_text: Source code, scripts, configuration files, and database schemas for in-scope systems are maintained in version control repositories. All changes are committed with descriptive messages linked to change tickets. Baseline versions are tagged and maintained for each production release. Unauthorized modifications to source code or configurations are detectable through version control audit trails and integrity monitoring.
evidence_requirements: Commit history samples with ticket references

controls.title: Patch Management
controls.description: Security patches and updates for in-scope systems are evaluated, tested, approved, and deployed within defined timeframes based on risk severity.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains a patch management process for all in-scope operating systems, databases, middleware, and applications. Security patches are evaluated for applicability and risk upon release by vendors. Critical and high-severity patches are deployed within defined SLAs (e.g., 30 days for critical, 90 days for high). Patches are tested before production deployment, and deployment is tracked and verified for completeness.
evidence_requirements: Patch management policy with SLA definitions
Patch deployment reports showing compliance with SLAs

controls.title: Job Scheduling and Batch Processing
controls.description: Automated batch jobs and scheduled processes critical to financial reporting are defined, authorized, monitored, and operate as intended.
controls.full_text: Batch jobs, scheduled tasks, and automated processes that support financial reporting (e.g., interface feeds, calculation engines, reconciliation jobs, report generation) are defined in a job scheduling system. Job schedules are documented, authorized by the application or business owner, and monitored for successful completion. Job failures or abnormal terminations are detected, alerted, investigated, and resolved in a timely manner with documented resolution.
evidence_requirements: Job monitoring dashboard or alert configuration
Job failure logs with resolution documentation
Sample of successful job completion evidence

controls.title: Data Backup and Recovery
controls.description: Data backup procedures ensure that financial data and system configurations are regularly backed up and can be restored within defined recovery objectives.
controls.full_text: The organization implements and maintains data backup procedures for all in-scope systems. Backups are performed according to a defined schedule (daily incremental, weekly full, or as appropriate) and include application data, databases, system configurations, and transaction logs. Backup media is stored securely, with copies maintained off-site or in a separate availability zone. Backup completion is monitored daily, and failures are investigated and remediated. Recovery point objectives (RPO) and recovery time objectives (RTO) are defined for each critical system.
evidence_requirements: Off-site or remote backup storage evidence

controls.title: Backup Restoration Testing
controls.description: Backup restoration is tested periodically to verify that backups are recoverable and meet defined recovery objectives.
controls.full_text: Restoration tests are performed periodically (at least annually, preferably quarterly) for in-scope systems to verify that backup data can be successfully restored within defined RTO and RPO parameters. Test results are documented, including the time required for restoration, data integrity validation, and any issues encountered. Failed restoration tests are escalated and remediated.
evidence_requirements: Remediation records for failed restoration tests

controls.title: System and Infrastructure Monitoring
controls.description: IT infrastructure and application systems are monitored for availability, performance, and capacity to ensure continuous operation of financial reporting systems.
controls.full_text: Monitoring tools and processes are implemented to track the availability, performance, and capacity of servers, databases, networks, and applications supporting financial reporting. Alerts are configured for critical thresholds (e.g., disk space, CPU utilization, memory usage, application response times). Monitoring alerts are reviewed and responded to in a timely manner. Infrastructure capacity is periodically assessed to ensure systems can support financial processing requirements.
evidence_requirements: Monitoring tool configuration and alert thresholds
Sample alert response and resolution records

controls.title: Incident Management for IT Operations
controls.description: IT incidents affecting financially significant systems are identified, classified, escalated, resolved, and documented through a formal incident management process.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains an IT incident management process for handling system outages, application errors, data corruption, and other operational events affecting in-scope systems. Incidents are logged in a ticketing system, classified by severity, and escalated based on defined criteria. Root cause analysis is performed for significant incidents, and corrective actions are tracked to completion. Incident trends are reported to management periodically.
evidence_requirements: Incident tickets with classification and resolution
Root cause analysis reports for significant incidents

controls.title: Disaster Recovery Planning
controls.description: Disaster recovery plans are maintained and tested for in-scope IT systems to ensure financial reporting can continue in the event of a significant disruption.
controls.full_text: Disaster recovery plans are documented for all in-scope IT systems and infrastructure supporting financial reporting. Plans include recovery procedures, communication protocols, alternative processing arrangements, roles and responsibilities, and vendor contact information. DR plans are reviewed and updated at least annually and tested at least annually through tabletop exercises, simulation tests, or full failover tests. Test results are documented and gaps remediated.
evidence_requirements: Annual DR plan review and update evidence
DR test plans and execution results
Gap remediation tracking from DR tests

controls.title: Interface and Data Transfer Controls
controls.description: Automated data interfaces and transfers between financially significant systems are controlled, monitored, and reconciled to ensure completeness and accuracy.
controls.full_text: Automated interfaces that transfer data between in-scope systems (e.g., sub-ledger to general ledger, payroll to GL, bank feeds, intercompany transactions) are documented with source, destination, frequency, and expected record counts. Interface processing is monitored for successful completion, and reconciliation controls verify completeness and accuracy of transferred data. Interface failures trigger alerts and are investigated and resolved timely.
evidence_requirements: Interface failure investigation and resolution records

controls.title: SDLC Policy and Methodology
controls.description: A formal software development lifecycle (SDLC) methodology governs the development of new applications and major enhancements to existing financially significant systems.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains a documented SDLC methodology that governs the development of new applications and significant modifications to existing systems supporting financial reporting. The methodology defines phases including requirements gathering, design, development, testing, deployment, and post-implementation review. Security requirements and control design considerations are integrated into each phase. The methodology is reviewed and updated periodically.
evidence_requirements: SDLC methodology review and update records

controls.title: Requirements Definition and Approval
controls.description: Business and functional requirements for new systems or significant changes are formally documented, reviewed, and approved by business stakeholders.
controls.full_text: Business requirements and functional specifications for new system development or major enhancements are formally documented and include data integrity requirements, control requirements, security requirements, and regulatory compliance requirements. Requirements are reviewed and approved by business owners, project sponsors, and relevant stakeholders before development begins. Changes to approved requirements follow a formal change control process.
evidence_requirements: Traceability matrix linking requirements to design and testing

controls.title: System Design and Architecture Review
controls.description: System design and architecture are reviewed for security, data integrity, and control considerations before development begins.
controls.full_text: Design specifications and system architecture for new developments are reviewed by technical leads, security personnel, and relevant stakeholders to ensure that data integrity controls, security requirements, audit logging, and regulatory requirements are incorporated. Design reviews are documented, and identified issues are tracked to resolution before development proceeds.

controls.title: Development and Testing Standards
controls.description: Development follows secure coding standards, and comprehensive testing validates that the system meets requirements and operates correctly.
controls.full_text: Development activities follow documented coding standards that address secure coding practices, error handling, input validation, and code quality. Testing is performed at multiple levels including unit testing, system integration testing, performance testing, security testing, and user acceptance testing (UAT). Test plans cover positive and negative test scenarios. Business users validate and formally accept the system before production deployment.
evidence_requirements: Test plans covering all testing phases
Test execution results and defect tracking

controls.title: Data Conversion and Migration Controls
controls.description: Data migration and conversion activities during new system implementations are controlled to ensure completeness, accuracy, and integrity of migrated financial data.
controls.full_text: Data migration and conversion from legacy systems to new systems follow a documented migration plan that includes data mapping, transformation rules, validation procedures, and reconciliation steps. Pre- and post-migration reconciliations are performed to verify completeness and accuracy of converted data. Data integrity checks validate that balances, transaction counts, and key financial data are accurately migrated. Migration issues are tracked and resolved before go-live.
evidence_requirements: Data migration plan and mapping documentation
Migration issue tracking and resolution records

controls.title: Post-Implementation Review
controls.description: Post-implementation reviews are conducted after go-live to verify that the new system operates as intended and controls are functioning effectively.
controls.full_text: Following the production deployment of new systems or major enhancements, post-implementation reviews are conducted to validate that the system meets approved requirements, controls are operating effectively, performance is acceptable, and no unintended impacts to financial reporting processes have occurred. Lessons learned are documented and fed back into the SDLC methodology. Outstanding issues from implementation are tracked to resolution.

controls.title: Environment Segregation
controls.description: Development, testing, and production environments are segregated to prevent unauthorized or untested changes from affecting production financial systems.
controls.full_text: Development, testing/QA, staging, and production environments are logically and/or physically separated. Developers do not have write access to production environments. Production data is not used in development/test environments without appropriate masking or anonymization of sensitive financial data. Access to each environment is controlled independently, and configuration differences between environments are documented and managed.
evidence_requirements: Data masking procedures for non-production use

controls.title: End-User Computing Inventory and Risk Assessment
controls.description: Spreadsheets, databases, and other end-user computing (EUC) tools used in financial reporting are inventoried and assessed for risk.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains an inventory of end-user computing tools (spreadsheets, Access databases, macros, scripts, and other user-developed applications) that are used in financially significant processes. Each EUC is assessed for risk based on complexity, materiality, frequency of use, and degree of manual manipulation. High-risk EUCs are subject to enhanced controls including version control, access restrictions, and independent review.
evidence_requirements: EUC inventory listing with risk ratings
High-risk EUC identification and enhanced control documentation

controls.title: Spreadsheet and EUC Access Controls
controls.description: Access to critical spreadsheets and EUC tools is restricted to authorized users, and changes are controlled.
controls.full_text: Access to critical EUC files (spreadsheets, databases, macros) is restricted through file-level permissions, folder access controls, or SharePoint/shared drive permissions. Write access is limited to authorized preparers and reviewers. Formulas and macros in critical spreadsheets are protected from unauthorized modification through cell protection, workbook protection, or macro signing. Change history or version tracking is enabled for critical EUCs.
evidence_requirements: Version history or change tracking evidence
Access review documentation for EUC repositories

controls.title: EUC Validation and Review
controls.description: Formulas, calculations, and logic within critical EUC tools are validated and independently reviewed before use in financial reporting.
controls.full_text: Critical formulas, calculations, lookup tables, and logic within EUC tools used for financial reporting are validated upon initial creation and after any modification. Independent review is performed by someone other than the preparer to verify accuracy of formulas and calculations. Review evidence (reviewer initials, sign-off, review checklist) is documented. Periodic re-validation of critical EUCs is performed at least annually.

controls.title: EUC Input Data Validation
controls.description: Data input into critical EUC tools is validated for completeness and accuracy through input controls and reconciliation.
controls.full_text: Data inputs to critical EUC tools are validated through input controls such as data validation rules, dropdown lists, range checks, and completeness checks. Source data is reconciled to ensure all expected inputs have been captured. Manual data entry is minimized through automated data feeds where feasible. Input errors are detected and corrected before the EUC output is used for financial reporting purposes.

controls.title: IT Segregation of Duties Framework
controls.description: A segregation of duties framework defines incompatible IT functions and establishes controls to prevent inappropriate combinations of access.
controls.full_text: The organization defines and documents a segregation of duties (SoD) framework identifying incompatible combinations of IT functions and application access rights. Key IT SoD conflicts include: developer vs. production migrator, system administrator vs. security administrator, requester vs. approver, and preparer vs. reviewer. The framework covers both IT operational SoD and application-level SoD within financially significant applications. SoD conflicts are detected and prevented through access controls or mitigated through compensating controls.
evidence_requirements: SoD exception and compensating control documentation

controls.title: Application-Level Segregation of Duties
controls.description: Application security configurations enforce segregation of duties for critical business transactions within financially significant applications.
controls.full_text: Security roles and access profiles within in-scope financial applications (ERP, GL, sub-ledgers, treasury, procurement) are designed to enforce segregation of duties for incompatible business functions such as: journal entry creation vs. approval, vendor master creation vs. payment processing, purchase order creation vs. goods receipt, and payroll processing vs. payroll approval. SoD conflicts are analyzed using role-based access analysis, and identified violations are remediated or mitigated.
evidence_requirements: Application role design documentation with SoD considerations
SoD conflict analysis reports (e.g., GRC tool output)
Compensating control documentation for accepted SoD conflicts

controls.title: Periodic SoD Review and Monitoring
controls.description: Segregation of duties compliance is reviewed periodically, and new SoD conflicts are identified and addressed through ongoing monitoring.
controls.full_text: Periodic reviews (at least semi-annually) are performed to identify and assess segregation of duties conflicts across IT functions and within financial applications. Reviews analyze current user access assignments against the SoD conflict matrix. New conflicts identified through access changes are flagged and addressed through access modification or implementation of compensating controls. SoD monitoring results are reported to management.
evidence_requirements: Management sign-off on SoD review results
Trend analysis of SoD conflicts over time

controls.title: IT Governance and Oversight
controls.description: Management has established IT governance structures that provide oversight of IT risks, strategy, and controls supporting financial reporting.
controls.full_text: The organization has established IT governance structures (IT steering committee, IT risk committee, or equivalent) that provide oversight of IT strategy, risk management, and internal controls relevant to financial reporting. Governance bodies meet regularly, review key IT metrics and risk indicators, approve significant IT investments and changes, and monitor the status of IT control deficiencies and remediation efforts. IT governance activities are documented through meeting minutes and action tracking.
evidence_requirements: IT risk dashboard and metrics reviewed

controls.title: IT Risk Assessment
controls.description: The organization performs periodic IT risk assessments to identify and evaluate risks to IT systems supporting financial reporting.
controls.full_text: The organization conducts periodic IT risk assessments (at least annually) to identify, evaluate, and prioritize risks to IT systems, applications, and infrastructure supporting financial reporting. Risk assessments consider threats including unauthorized access, data integrity failures, system availability issues, cybersecurity threats, and third-party risks. Risk assessment results inform the SOX scoping process, control design, and audit planning. Identified risks are documented in a risk register and tracked through mitigation activities.
evidence_requirements: IT risk register with risk ratings and mitigation plans
Risk assessment review and approval by management

controls.title: IT Policy Framework
controls.description: A comprehensive IT policy framework is established, approved, communicated, and periodically reviewed covering all key IT control domains.
controls.full_text: The organization maintains a comprehensive set of IT policies covering information security, access management, change management, data management, acceptable use, incident management, business continuity, and third-party risk management. Policies are approved by appropriate management, communicated to all relevant personnel, and reviewed and updated at least annually. Policy compliance is monitored and policy violations are addressed through defined escalation procedures.
evidence_requirements: IT policy inventory and approval records

controls.title: IT Security Awareness and Training
controls.description: IT security awareness training is provided to all employees, with specialized training for IT personnel with SOX-relevant responsibilities.
controls.full_text: The organization provides IT security awareness training to all employees at hire and periodically thereafter (at least annually). Training covers information security policies, acceptable use, phishing awareness, data protection, and incident reporting. Additional specialized training is provided to IT personnel responsible for SOX-relevant controls, covering their specific control responsibilities, change management procedures, and access management requirements. Training completion is tracked and non-compliance is escalated.

controls.title: IT Audit and Self-Assessment
controls.description: Periodic IT audits and self-assessments evaluate the design and operating effectiveness of IT controls supporting financial reporting.
controls.full_text: Internal audit or an equivalent function performs periodic assessments of IT general controls covering all ITGC domains (access management, change management, computer operations, program development). Assessments evaluate both design effectiveness (controls are appropriately designed to mitigate identified risks) and operating effectiveness (controls have operated consistently throughout the period). Findings and deficiencies are documented, risk-rated, and reported to management. Remediation plans with target dates are developed and tracked.
evidence_requirements: Remediation action plans with status tracking

controls.title: Third-Party and Service Organization Management
controls.description: Third-party service providers that process, store, or transmit data relevant to financial reporting are assessed and monitored for appropriate controls.
controls.full_text: Third-party service providers (cloud providers, SaaS vendors, outsourced IT operations, managed service providers) that support financially significant applications or processes are identified and assessed for risk. SOC 1 or SOC 2 reports are obtained and reviewed annually for service organizations in scope. Complementary user entity controls (CUECs) identified in SOC reports are evaluated and implemented. Contract provisions require the service provider to maintain appropriate controls and provide audit rights. Vendor performance and control effectiveness are monitored ongoing.
evidence_requirements: SOC report review documentation with CUEC assessment
Contract provisions covering audit rights and control requirements

controls.title: Security Logging and Monitoring
controls.description: Security events and access activities on in-scope systems are logged, retained, and monitored for unauthorized or suspicious activity.
controls.full_text: Security-relevant events on in-scope systems (successful/failed logins, privilege escalations, configuration changes, data access, administrative actions) are logged and retained for a defined period (at least one year, with 90 days immediately available). Logs are protected from tampering and unauthorized access. Security monitoring processes or tools (SIEM) analyze logs for suspicious activity, unauthorized access attempts, and policy violations. Alerts are investigated and documented. Log review is performed periodically for systems without automated monitoring.
evidence_requirements: Log retention policy and evidence of retention
Security alert investigation and resolution records

controls.title: Financial Close Process Documentation
controls.description: The financial close process is documented with defined timelines, responsibilities, procedures, and IT system dependencies.
controls.full_text: The organization documents the financial close process including all steps, timelines, responsible parties, system dependencies, and control checkpoints. IT system dependencies (batch jobs, report generation, interface processing, system access requirements) for each close step are identified and documented. Close calendars define deadlines and dependencies between sub-processes. The close process documentation is reviewed and updated at least annually.
evidence_requirements: Close calendar with deadlines and dependencies

controls.title: Automated Calculations and Processing Controls
controls.description: Automated calculations, allocations, and processing logic within financial applications are configured correctly and validated.
controls.full_text: Automated calculations, allocations, currency conversions, depreciation schedules, accrual computations, and other processing logic within in-scope financial applications are configured in accordance with approved accounting policies and business rules. Configuration of automated calculations is documented and changes follow the change management process. Periodic validation confirms that automated processing produces accurate results. Configurable parameters (e.g., tax rates, depreciation methods, allocation percentages) are reviewed and approved by authorized personnel.
evidence_requirements: Reconciliation of automated outputs to expected results

controls.title: System-Generated Reports (IPE) Completeness and Accuracy
controls.description: Information produced by the entity (IPE) including system-generated reports used as audit evidence are validated for completeness and accuracy.
controls.full_text: System-generated reports, queries, and data extracts used as evidence for internal controls or relied upon for financial reporting decisions (Information Produced by the Entity - IPE) are validated for completeness and accuracy. Validation procedures include: verifying report parameters and selection criteria, reconciling report totals to independent sources, validating population completeness through record counts or hash totals, and documenting the basis for reliance. IPE validation is performed each time the report is used as control evidence.
evidence_requirements: Completeness and accuracy testing documentation per report

controls.title: Journal Entry Controls
controls.description: IT controls support the integrity of journal entry processing including automated posting, access restrictions, and approval workflows.
controls.full_text: IT controls support the journal entry process by enforcing: segregation of duties between journal entry preparer and approver through application security, restricting access to post journal entries to authorized personnel, maintaining a complete audit trail of all journal entries (preparer, approver, date, amounts, descriptions), enforcing mandatory fields and validation rules for journal entries, and supporting management review of unusual or non-standard journal entries through system-generated exception reports.
evidence_requirements: Exception reporting configuration for unusual entries

controls.title: Account Reconciliation System Controls
controls.description: IT systems supporting account reconciliation processes maintain data integrity and provide complete and accurate reconciliation data.
controls.full_text: IT controls ensure that account reconciliation processes are supported by reliable system data. Sub-ledger to general ledger reconciliation data is generated accurately from source systems. Reconciliation tools and applications enforce workflow requirements including preparer assignment, reviewer assignment, due dates, and escalation of unresolved items. System access to reconciliation tools is controlled with appropriate provisioning and review. Automated matching algorithms are validated for accuracy.
evidence_requirements: Sub-ledger to GL reconciliation system configuration

controls.title: Financial Reporting System Access
controls.description: Access to financial reporting tools, consolidation systems, and reporting databases is restricted to authorized personnel with appropriate approval.
controls.full_text: Access to financial consolidation systems, reporting databases, XBRL tagging tools, and financial disclosure preparation systems is restricted to authorized personnel in the financial reporting, accounting, and IT support functions. Access is provisioned through formal request and approval processes, reviewed periodically, and revoked upon termination or role change. Write access to reported financial data within consolidation and reporting systems is limited to authorized financial reporting personnel.
evidence_requirements: Access provisioning approvals for reporting systems
Periodic access review documentation for reporting tools

controls.title: Period-End Processing Controls
controls.description: IT controls ensure that period-end processing (close jobs, cutoff procedures, accrual processing) is executed completely and accurately.
controls.full_text: IT controls support period-end processing by ensuring that all required close batch jobs (sub-ledger closes, allocation runs, revaluation processing, consolidation) are executed in the correct sequence and complete successfully. Cutoff controls in financial applications prevent or detect transactions posted to incorrect periods. Period lock controls restrict posting to closed periods. Processing schedules are documented, monitored, and validated for completeness each period.
evidence_requirements: Batch job completion evidence for close processes
